questions.question: What is the primary distinction between an Application-Specific Integrated Circuit (ASIC) and a Field-Programmable Gate Array (FPGA)?
options: ASICs are reprogrammable after manufacturing, while FPGAs are not.
ASICs are designed for general-purpose computing, FPGAs for specialized tasks.
ASICs are custom-designed for a specific function and fabricated, while FPGAs are off-the-shelf reconfigurable devices.
FPGAs offer higher performance and lower power consumption compared to ASICs for the same function.
FPGAs are purely analog circuits, whereas ASICs are purely digital.

questions.question: In the physical design flow of an Integrated Circuit, what is the primary objective of the 'placement' step?
options: To determine the initial floorplan and aspect ratio of the chip.
To distribute clock signals uniformly across the chip to minimize skew.
To assign precise locations for all standard cells and macros on the chip area.
To connect all logic gates and I/O pins with metallic interconnects.
To extract parasitic capacitances and resistances from the layout for timing analysis.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the fundamental principle behind Dynamic Voltage and Frequency Scaling (DVFS) in modern processors and SoCs?
options: Completely shutting off power to idle functional blocks to save static leakage.
Adjusting the clock signal's duty cycle to control power consumption without changing frequency.
Varying the operating voltage and clock frequency of a circuit block to trade off performance for power consumption.
Using multiple, fixed voltage domains across different parts of the chip.
Temporarily halting instruction execution during periods of low activity.

questions.question: In modern multi-core processors, what is the primary role of a Memory Management Unit (MMU) in conjunction with a virtual memory system?
options: To manage the coherence of data between multiple processor caches.
To translate virtual memory addresses generated by the CPU into physical memory addresses.
To arbitrate access to the main memory bus among multiple cores.
To accelerate the execution of floating-point arithmetic operations.
To schedule tasks and threads across different processor cores.

questions.question: What is the critical challenge that a Synchronizer circuit is primarily designed to address when transferring signals between two asynchronous clock domains?
options: Reducing static power consumption in the receiving clock domain.
Ensuring that data is transferred at the highest possible bandwidth.
Preventing metastability when a signal transitions asynchronously relative to a receiving clock edge.
Filtering out high-frequency noise from the incoming signal.
Matching the impedance of the signal lines to prevent reflections.
